briefdescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous error interrupt callback.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler. On initialization, Error interrupt handler is set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCSI_ISR_*_MASK values defined in xcsi_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XCsi2Tx instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific CSI.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function will do a reset of the IP.

detaileddescription.para: This will reset the values of all registers except Core Config and Protocol Config registers.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE on timeout and core being stuck in reset

briefdescription.para: This function will enable/disable the IP Core to start processing.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.
will be used to indicate Enable or Disable action

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful core enable or disable
XST_FAILURE if core disable times out.

briefdescription.para: This function will configure the core with proper number of Active Lanes.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if active lanes not set correctly

briefdescription.para: This function will get the short packet received in the FIFO from the Generic Short Packet Register and fill up the structure passed from caller.

parameterdescription.para: is the XCsi2Tx instance to operate on
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the active lanes parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is the lane count to check if valid.

listitem.para: 1 if specified Active Lanes is valid.
0 otherwise, if the Active Lanes specified isn't valid as per spec and design.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

detaileddescription.para: This is to be called before starting the core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is valid line count for the Virtual channel.

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
XST_FAILURE in case the core is already running.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the Line Count for virtual Channel if Frame End Generation feature is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
is which Virtual channel to be configured for (0-3).
is pointer to variable to be filled with line count for the Virtual channel

listitem.para: XST_NO_FEATURE if Frame End generation is not enabled
XST_INVALID_PARAM if any param is invalid e.g. VC is always 0 to 3 and Line Count is 0 to 0xFFFF.
